title: Why did Kaworu think hed find Adam at Terminal Dogma
display_title: Why did Kaworu think hed find Adam at Terminal Dogma
wikitext:
[]: Why did Kaworu think he'd find Adam at Terminal Dogma?             Quote Post by Kaz » Wed Nov 25, 2009 8:14 am I may be mistaken, but as i recall it, the angels tried to invade the Geofront to eventually enter the Terminal Dogma and reunite with Lilith, for a reason that i dont really understand. But nevermind, that's not my question. What confuses me is this- Why did Kaworu expect to find Adam at the Terminal Dogma when all the other angels obviously knew that it was Lilith? How could he make that mistake?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Nov 25, 2009 8:42 am because Anno is troll and creating huge plothole of doom was fine way to troll people

[]: Re: Why did Kaworu think he'd find Adam at Terminal Dogma?             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Wed Nov 25, 2009 8:49 am Kaz wrote: What confuses me is this- Why did Kaworu expect to find Adam at the Terminal Dogma when all the other angels obviously knew that it was Lilith? How could he make that mistake?

[]: Re: Why did Kaworu think he'd find Adam at Terminal Dogma?             Quote Post by Kaz » Wed Nov 25, 2009 9:01 am Sharaz Destler wrote: Trust me, I'm half expecting a black monolith pop up in Terminal Dogma during that scene and say, "Smile, Kaworu Nagisa, you're on Candid Camera !" in the final installment of Rebuild .

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Nov 25, 2009 10:30 am Actually, Kaworu carries the soul of Adam within himself (as far as we know). Seele probably told him that "Your real body is down below Nerv Japan's headquarters. Fetch it and all shall be awesome." As to why Seele did that: i think they wanted to put Humanity to an ultimate test of sort: if Nerv can kill the Final Angel, which is actually the First Angel, then humankind is worthy of Instrumentality. If not... well, then it doesn't really matter for Seele from that point on. Also, crippling Nerv from the inside must have been a nice bonus, should they (Nerv) try to pull anything funny (as they did) in the future.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 25, 2009 11:03 am Kaji was a spy for the Japanese government, right? And Seele had its hands in the various world governments, right? So it's not unreasonable that Kaji found Lilith, thinking it was Adam, reported back, Seele got the tip, and were all like, "YO KAWORU, CHECK IT. GO SNEAK THAT SHIT".

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 25, 2009 11:18 am EM: "OKAY." Somebody remind me again why the Adam/Lilith stuff HAS to be a plot-hole? Is it because how Seele to Kaworu in DC24 states "lol Ikari has Adam's body but you have her soul"? Excluding that, it's not surprising for Kaji to somehow think the Adam embryo grew to become a giant marshmellow, unless he DID know Lilith was Lilith and lied to Misato for whatever reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Nov 25, 2009 11:32 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Somebody remind me again why the Adam/Lilith stuff HAS to be a plot-hole? Is it because how Seele to Kaworu in DC24 states "lol Ikari has Adam's body but you have her soul"?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 25, 2009 11:53 am Yojimbo's theory of Kaworu having a silent reveation is nice, but it's still a stretch.

[]: Re: Why did Kaworu think he'd find Adam at Terminal Dogma?             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Nov 25, 2009 12:32 pm Kaz wrote: oh and wait a second, Made ? What do you mean by that? How could SEELE "make" Kaworu? By being the 17th angel, isn't it a fact he is a spawn of Adam?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaz » Wed Nov 25, 2009 1:25 pm I never understood that. When exactly in the series do we learn that Kaworu contains Adam's Soul? The first time i heard about it was a couple of days ago when i read through the Wiki. From what i remember the only things we were told about him was the he was sent to Nerv by SEELE, he is "Like Rei", and that he's classified as the 17th in the computers. Did i miss/forget anything? :O

[]: Re: Why did Kaworu think he'd find Adam at Terminal Dogma?             Quote Post by Rodeo » Wed Nov 25, 2009 1:36 pm Kaz wrote: ...when all the other angels obviously knew that it was Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 25, 2009 2:19 pm What confuses me is this- Why did Kaworu expect to find Adam at the Terminal Dogma when all the other angels obviously knew that it was Lilith? How could he make that mistake?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Nov 25, 2009 2:20 pm Kaz wrote: I never understood that. When exactly in the series do we learn that Kaworu contains Adam's Soul? The first time i heard about it was a couple of days ago when i read through the Wiki. From what i remember the only things we were told about him was the he was sent to Nerv by SEELE, he is "Like Rei", and that he's classified as the 17th in the computers. Did i miss/forget anything? :O

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Nov 25, 2009 2:31 pm Actually, what the Angels were after varied from Angel to Angel. according to the C.I: Some of them were trying to access Lilith and reset all life, some of them had nothing in mind, and some were trying to recover their progenitor Adam. The Angels — Adam-based life — became active under their respective tactics for survival and success.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 25, 2009 3:35 pm Some of them were trying to access Lilith and reset all life, some of them had nothing in mind, and some were trying to recover their progenitor Adam. The Angels — Adam-based life — became active under their respective tactics for survival and success.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Nov 25, 2009 4:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: What makes you think the other Angels knew it was Lilith? They indicate no such thing, and since they're going after it with the intent of uniting with their Mother, I would wager they're making the same error.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Nov 25, 2009 8:45 pm Relevant links: Readable version which condenses the theories discussed in this monster thread. The latter of which nearly drained me of all of my fanwank powers. Abandon all hope ye who enter there. There were also some really good (and much shorter) threads that cropped up recently about that controversy which went a long way to unraveling the entire mess. I wish I could remember the titles...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Nov 25, 2009 10:49 pm AuraTwlight: The CI is in Eva2. NGE64 is a different game. Wayneiac: Interesting. Is his theory in any ANF archeve threads?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Nov 25, 2009 11:49 pm Look closely, Star Dust. I said "Neon Genesis Evangelion 64 2 game."
